title: clusia peninsulae
intro: Clusia peninsulae, also known as the Clusia tree or the "live fence", is a species of flowering plant in the Clusiaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Clusia peninsulae include its ability to treat various conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Herbal preparations of Clusia peninsulae can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the roots and bark to create a tincture or decoction.
